subject: Biology
title: Sex Determination Across Species
description: A 60-second lesson on Sex Determination Across Species.

hook.text: In humans, chromosomes decide sex. In crocodiles, temperature does. Evolution invented many methods.

buildup.text: Mammals use XX/XY chromosomes. Birds use ZW/ZZ. Many reptiles use nest temperature.

discovery.text: Clownfish are all born male. When the dominant female dies, the largest male becomes female.

twist.text: Some whiptail lizard species are all female—reproducing by parthenogenesis without any males at all.

climax.text: Sex determination systems evolve rapidly. Even closely related species can use different mechanisms.

punchline.text: Male, female, or neither. Nature decides many ways.

quiz.question: What determines sex in many reptiles?
